solution: 
```class Solution:
    def defangIPaddr(self, address: str) -> str:
        return address.replace('.', '[.]')
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses Python's built-in string method `replace()` to efficiently replace all occurrences of '.' with '[.]' in the input string, resulting in a defanged IP address. The time complexity is O(n) where n is the length of the input string, and the space complexity is also O(n) as we create a new string with the replaced characters. The energy implications are low as this solution only involves basic string operations.
